transcript.clean_text: it's stage nine of the giro d'italia here on gcn and eurosport and the biggest test of the race so far 3 000 meters of climbing over 158 kilometers four categorized climbs along the way with plenty more ascending though to be done between them it was the one to the finish at campo felicia that we were all the most intrigued by the final 1.6 kilometers of it on a gravel road with an 8.8 percent average gradient and one pitch at 14 percent it took a long time for the break to form today so long in fact that even some gc threats were getting involved damiana caruso here making his way across to a large group over the top of the paso gerdi the points there taken by teammate maida with two teammates for company they immediately set to work but then disaster struck an awful crash for mati maharaj of bahrain victorious had us all fearing the worst thankfully he was soon back on his feet but the severity of the incident hadn't yet hit him he was taken away in an ambulance to assess his injuries and of course would wish him a speedy recovery the race continued though and after 70k is a full-on attack and we finally had a break 17 riders the best placed on gc tanau kangert at 5 minutes and 37. group armor fdj had been set in the pace but inside the last 30 case it was ineos grenadiers who'd hit the front and with the bunch closing in simon carr of ef education nippo decided to set sail from the break he'd soon have company though frenchman jeffrey bouchard of 82 i've been racking up the mountain points over the stage and over the penultimate climb of the day he'd gone into the virtual lead in that competition and he was clearly on a very good day a few days down the road he drove away from car his eyes firmly now set on stage victory behind him kuhn bauman of jumbo bismuth would set off in pursuit 31 seconds the initial gap he'd have to close and closing on the last one kilometer of the race two riders were now out front behind ineos was setting a ferocious pace moscone drilling it on the front burnell glued to his wheel vlasov of astana was one of the first gc riders to go on the attack on that final climb but then bernal let off a bomb flicking it up onto the big ring he would go past bauman and bouchard like they were absolutely stood still chicony stuck with him for a few brief moments but bernal was going to have it all his own way today he would continue sprinting all the way to the finish line and eventually cross it to take his first ever grand tour stage victory wow jocconi would cling on to take second place on the day in front of vlassoff seven seconds behind burn now and it was a very three seconds before even nepal and dan martin stopped the clock in fourth and fifth respectively the rest of the gc favorites not too far behind though can't say more about this man though again burnout back on the best form that we've seen him in in quite some time this is the rest of the top ten caruso barde soleil martinez and almeida filling it out and they'd come across the line in a group that also contained karthi yates and davide formula meanwhile attila valter would cross the line 49 seconds after burnell in 25th place his stint in pink over but i've got no doubt we'll see plenty more of that man in the days and years to come we've got a new leader of the gear at italia though again bernal was on the podium collecting the first pink jersey of his career and who'd bet against him taking it all the way to milan a lot of racing before we get there though of course and the gaps are still small 15 seconds back to avenue 21 to vlasov judo taconi now up to fourth whilst vouter slips out of the race lead and down to fifth still though just over one minute separating burnout from caruso in 10th so a lot still to play for tomorrow it should be a little bit more relaxing for the riders than today the shortest road stage of the race at 139 kilometers starting out from l'aquila it's a hard one to predict there is some climbing with a fourth cap peaking with 39 k's to go under normal circumstances you'd expect it to be a sprint the question is who's going to control the race the finish into fellini is flat but let us know what you think breakaway or sprint join us tomorrow on g sim plus to find out territory restrictions do apply but we hope you're able to join us then
